player.selectInstrumentHint: Select an instrument to configure it

tips.bpm: Playback speed in beats per minute.
tips.transpose: Shift all notes up or down by this many semitones.
tips.noteWidth: How wide each note appears in the visualizer. Smaller = more notes visible at once.
tips.start: Countdown in seconds before playback begins.
tips.latency: Audio latency compensation. Increase if the visuals appear ahead of the audio.
tips.particles: Show or hide the particle effects that emit from active notes.
tips.pulse: Animate instrument buttons when they trigger a note.
tips.ambient: Shows a coloured glow behind the page that reacts to note triggers.
tips.ecoMode: Reduces audio effects (reverb, vibrato, chorus) and visual effects (glow, particles, anti-aliasing) for better performance on lower-end and mobile devices. Automatically detected based on your device's hardware.

rangeWarning.default: Some notes in this track fall outside the instrument's playable range.
rangeWarning.impossible: No available fingering system can cover all notes in this track.

hint.recorderType: Switched to {{type}} recorder - suggested by this song

tips.vibrato: Adds a vibrato effect to sustained notes.
tips.variant: Switches between different recorded sample sets.
tips.type: Selects the recorder type (soprano, alto, tenor, bass). Each type covers a different pitch range - most electronic woodwind instruments default to tenor.
tips.system: Selects the fingering chart system. 'Simple' is the layout used by most electronic woodwind instruments and, together with tenor, covers the widest playable range.

tips.volume: Adjusts the accompaniment recorder playback volume.
tips.vibrato: Adjusts the vibrato depth of the accompaniment recorder.
tips.variant: Switches between different recorder sample sets.

about.title: About SynRecordia
about.intro: SynRecordia started as a personal experiment - I wanted to see how far I could push music visualization in the browser. Inspired by <synthesia>Synthesia</synthesia>, I built a lightweight web app where you can watch fingering and note labels update in real time as the song plays.

whereFrom.title: Where it came from
whereFrom.text: This is a rewrite of an older project of mine, <recorderVis>RecorderVisualization</recorderVis>, which I originally built in GameMaker Studio 2. Moving it to the web gave me much better control over precision, performance, and who can actually use it.

stack.title: The stack
stack.text: The visualizer runs on PIXI.js for WebGL rendering and Tone.js for sample-accurate audio. React handles all the state and UI logic on top.

inProgress.title: Still in progress
inProgress.practiceModeText: Real-time note detection so you can play along and get feedback.

knownIssues.title: Known issues
knownIssues.mobilePerf: Audio might be distorted on mobile devices due to phone performance, I'm trying to fix.
knownIssues.notesOutOfRange: A few songs have notes that fall outside the sample range, so they may sound off or silent.
knownIssues.playmodeInput: Playmode input responsiveness is terrible, I'm improving it.

aboutMe.title: About me & source
aboutMe.text: I'm a developer who likes building things at the intersection of frontend engineering and creative tools. SynRecordia is how I've been learning web audio and high-performance canvas rendering - and it's fully open source. If you want to contribute, especially around audio or rendering, I'd love that.

q1.question: 1. What kind of recorder does this work with?
q1.answer: SynRecordia works with any recorder - soprano, alto, tenor, or bass. It was built with electronic woodwind instruments in mind: the one used during development is the Carry-On Simple recorder, a digital EWI-style instrument. It's no longer for sale, but the second edition is available on their <carryOn>website</carryOn>.

qTenor.question: What are the 'recorder type' and 'simple' settings about?
qTenor.answer: The recorder type (soprano, alto, tenor, bass) tells the visualizer which pitch range your instrument covers. The fingering system switches the hole diagram to a different layout - baroque and german are the two classical styles, while 'simple' is the scheme most electronic woodwind instruments use by default. Tenor + simple happens to cover the widest playable range of any combination, so if the app warns you that notes are out of range, that's usually the first thing to try.

q2.question: 2. What am I actually looking at?
q2.intro: The visualizer shows which recorder holes are being pressed for each note. The holes are split into three groups:
q2.group1: Group 1: the left-thumb hole (the back hole).
q2.group2: Group 2: three holes for the left hand - index, middle, and ring fingers.
q2.group3: Group 3: four holes for the right hand - index, middle, ring, and pinky fingers.
q2.outro: There's also a "?" button in the UI that overlays a diagram of the actual instrument so you can see how the holes map to the real thing.

q3.question: 3. How do I open a song?
q3.answer: Hit the folder button in the top-left, or open the directory right here:

q4.question: 4. Why is the audio played faster than the visualization?
q4.answer: This is a timing offset between the audio and the visuals. You can adjust it in the Advanced settings using the Latency Calibration slider.

q5.question: 5. Why do some notes have audio but no visual?
q5.answer: Some notes fall outside the fingerable range for the currently selected fingering system. Try switching to a different instrument system in the Instrument Controls.

q6.question: 6. Some fingering patterns are missing?
q6.answer: Some alternate fingerings from reference materials aren't in yet, but they're planned. You can switch between available fingering systems in the Instrument Controls. The chart was sourced from <chart>this recorder fingering reference</chart>.

q7.question: 7. Why is it lagging?
q7.answer: The most common cause is a very small note width - when the notes are tiny, a lot more of them are on screen at once. Try increasing the note width in the settings.

q8.question: 8. Can I load my own songs?
q8.answer: Not yet, but it's on the list. MIDI import is the priority - stay tuned.

q9.question: 9. Found a bug or have a feature idea?
q9.answer: Open an issue on <github>GitHub</github> or shoot me an email at <email>huuthang.l@outlook.com</email>. Include your browser/OS, what you were doing, and any console errors if you have them.
